trigger: operator 'continue further' — third anti-bluff invocation in same 24h window
protocol: §6.N.1.1 subsequent-invocation lighter hunt; 1-2 files most relevant to recent invocation context (Phase F.2.6 rutracker per-clone HttpClient just landed at 7ce19967)

targets.purpose: Hand-rolled object that constructs the full RuTracker subgraph from a caller-supplied HttpClient — the load-bearing seam that F.2.6's clone-URL routing depends on. Distinct from the factory-level override-strip mutation rehearsed in the original Bluff-Audit stamp.
targets.mutation: Replace `RuTrackerInnerApiImpl(httpClient)` with `RuTrackerInnerApiImpl(RuTrackerHttpClientFactory.create("https://rutracker.org/forum/"))` — i.e., the bluff 'ignored the passed httpClient and used a hardcoded production URL'.
targets.expected_failure_signal: RuTrackerClientFactoryCloneUrlTest's MockWebServer receives 0 requests because the request went to rutracker.org instead; `assertNotNull("no request reached the MockWebServer at the override URL")` fires.
targets.observed_failure: java.lang.AssertionError: no request reached the MockWebServer at the override URL — testsuite tests=2 failures=1 errors=0.
targets.rationale: The F.2.6 falsifiability anchor catches TWO distinct bluff vectors: (a) factory-level override-strip (singleton-error Provider fires), AND (b) subgraph-level httpClient-ignore (MockWebServer 0 captures). Both layers of the override propagation are independently provable.

targets.purpose: §5-follow-up redaction implementation just landed at 0df8c6cc. Verifies the redactAuthHeader function actually does the redaction rather than passing the raw value through.
targets.expected_failure_signal: NetworkLoggerRedactionTest's `assertFalse(captured.contains(rawHeaderValue))` and `assertFalse(captured.contains(rawValue))` fire across 2 of 3 test cases.
targets.observed_failure: java.lang.AssertionError: captured log line must NOT contain the raw auth value; was: REQUEST: ... Lava-Auth: kQHRzU7M2YwYjQwM2VmNTRkLW5vbi1zZWNyZXQtdGVzdC1maXh0dXJl ... — AND case-insensitive match must still redact.
targets.rationale: The redaction's only job is to strip the raw value from the log line; the negative assertion on the raw value's presence is the user-visible (operator-visible) signal that the leak is closed. Both case-sensitive and case-insensitive variants are independently falsifiable.

constitutional_compliance.§6.N.1.1: satisfied — third lighter hunt in same-24h window per cadence.
constitutional_compliance.§6.J: satisfied — both mutations target user-observable signals (MockWebServer wire capture, log-sink line content).
constitutional_compliance.§6.L: satisfied — addresses operator's 'continue further... enforce fully no-bluff policy' directive by performing independent layer-by-layer falsifiability rehearsals of just-landed F.2.6 + NetworkLogger code.
